# M3 Stage 1 Review Summary — API Plan

**Change**: `20260612-user-mgmt`
**Module**: `users`
**Stage**: AWS M3 Stage 1 (planning, no code generation)
**Generated by**: aws-api-plan (inline)
**Generated at**: 2026-06-12

---

## Overview

This M3 Stage 1 plan covers **15 API automation cases** for the `users` module, all marked `automation.required = true` in `qa/changes/20260612-user-mgmt/cases/users/case.yaml`. The plan converts case-level intent (under `case.yaml:added`) into reviewable HTTP, data, and codegen artifacts without generating any test code.

The plan is grounded in the verified fact baseline (`facts/fact-baseline.json`) and the project's documented data knowledge (`.aws/data-knowledge.yaml`). All required fixtures and helpers exist with `confidence: high`.

---

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|

| `plans/api-test-data-plan.md` | Per-case data setup, required entities, cleanup ordering, concurrency notes |
| `plans/api-codegen-plan.md` | Output file layout, function-to-case mapping, fixture wiring, forbidden practices |

## Cases In Scope (15)

| ID | Title | HTTP | Expected status |
|---|---|---|---|
| TC-USER-001 | List users — default pagination | GET `/user/list` | 200 |
| TC-USER-002 | List users — filter by username | GET `/user/list?username=...` | 200 |
| TC-USER-003 | List users — anonymous rejected | GET `/user/list` (no token) | 401 |
| TC-USER-010 | Get user — by ID | GET `/user/get?user_id=...` | 200 |
| TC-USER-011 | Get user — non-existent ID | GET `/user/get?user_id=999999` | 404 |
| TC-USER-020 | Create user — with role binding | POST `/user/create` | 200 |
| TC-USER-021 | Create user — duplicate email | POST `/user/create` | 400 |
| TC-USER-022 | Create user — missing email validation | POST `/user/create` | 422 |
| TC-USER-030 | Update user — rewrite roles | POST `/user/update` | 200 |
| TC-USER-031 | Update user — non-existent ID | POST `/user/update` | 404 |
| TC-USER-040 | Delete user — by ID | DELETE `/user/delete?user_id=...` | 200 |
| TC-USER-041 | Delete user — non-existent ID | DELETE `/user/delete?user_id=999999` | 404 |
| TC-USER-050 | Reset password — normal user | POST `/user/reset_password` | 200 |
| TC-USER-051 | Reset password — superuser blocked | POST `/user/reset_password` | 403 |
| TC-USER-052 | Reset password — non-existent | POST `/user/reset_password` | 404 |

**Out of scope** (per `case.yaml:removed` and proposal): None for API. All proposed API cases are present.

**Note**: `dept_id` filter listing (mentioned in early proposal drafts) was dropped before case.yaml finalization — confirmed absent here intentionally.

---

## Forward References Resolved

The plan applies all forward-reference resolutions from `facts/fact-baseline.json`:

- **Reset password literal**: `"123456"` (TC-USER-050) — sourced from `app/controllers/user.py:reset_password`.
- **Superuser block**: HTTP 403 + msg `"不允许重置超级管理员密码"` (TC-USER-051) — sourced from `app/controllers/user.py`.
- **Duplicate email msg**: `"The user with this email already exists in the system."` (TC-USER-021) — sourced from `app/controllers/user.py:create_user`.
- **Created/Updated/Deleted msgs**: `"Created Successfully"`, `"Updated Successfully"`, `"Deleted Successfully"` — sourced from controller return values.
- **DoesNotExist envelope**: HTTP 404, body `code=404`, msg starts with `"Object has not found"` — sourced from `app/core/exceptions.py:DoesNotExistHandle`.
- **Validation envelope**: HTTP 422, body `code=422`, msg starts with `"RequestValidationError, "` — sourced from `app/core/exceptions.py:RequestValidationHandle`.

---

## Auth Strategy Summary

- **14 authenticated cases**: `api_client` fixture (header `token: <admin_jwt>`), driven by default admin (`admin` / `123456`, `is_superuser=true`).
- **1 anonymous case** (TC-USER-003): `unauthenticated_client` fixture.
- **2 cases need secondary login** (TC-USER-050, TC-USER-051): direct `httpx.post` to `/api/v1/base/access_token` with `backend_base_url`.
- **Forbidden**: literal `"dev"` token, `Authorization: Bearer` header.

---

## Data Strategy Summary

- **Strategy**: Per-test isolation via existing factory fixtures + deterministic prefixed usernames (`qa_*`).
- **No persistent shared seed data**.
- **Cleanup**: `try/finally` per test + auto-cleanup fixtures. New helper `safe_delete_user_by_username` added to `tests/api/helpers/user_api.py` (3-line wrapper over two existing helpers).
- **Concurrency**: Tests serialize (sqlite single-writer); no `pytest-xdist`.
- **Hard rule**: Admin record never mutated (TC-USER-051 must not change admin password under any circumstance).

---

## Open Items for Reviewer

These are documented in the per-file `Needs Review` sections; consolidated here:

### Non-blocking (advisory)

1. **ORM-verify sync-wrapper idiom** (`api-codegen-plan.md`): Plan defers exact pattern to codegen-time inspection of `tests/api/test_role_api.py`. Reviewer can pre-supply the pattern or accept the deferral.
2. **TC-USER-022 message-shape brittleness** (`api-plan.md`): Plan asserts `"email" in body["msg"]`. If Pydantic v2 stringification format changes, assertion may need to relax. Reviewer can confirm tolerance.
3. **TC-USER-051 admin-isolation safeguard** (`api-codegen-plan.md`): Plan flags admin as never-modify but does not add an autouse sentinel. Reviewer can opt to add session-scoped post-test admin login check.
4. **Parametrize style preference** (`api-codegen-plan.md`): 15 functions, no `parametrize`. Reviewer confirms style.
5. **Pre-test purge pattern** (`api-test-data-plan.md`): Per-test `safe_delete_user_by_username` calls vs session-scoped autouse purge. Plan chose per-test. Reviewer confirms.

### Blockers

**None.**

---

## Phase Gate

This plan is ready for **aws-api-plan-reviewer** (Phase 5A).

If reviewer returns `decision=pass` with `codegen_readiness in ["ready", "ready_with_warnings"]`, codegen (Phase 7A) may proceed.

If reviewer returns `decision=needs_fix` with `auto_fix_allowed=true`, **aws-api-plan-fixer** (Phase 6A) loops up to 2 iterations.

If reviewer returns `decision=blocked` or `human_review_required=true`, escalation to user is required.
